summary: Cleaned up configure.py by removing duplicate Object entries where the same source file was listed as both Matching and NonMatching in build library definitions. The PR also applied small formatting cleanups to imports, VERSIONS, and one long Object call. This mattered because duplicate library/object declarations can make progress accounting and build configuration ambiguous: a file should have a single configured matching state at a time.
what_changed: Removed duplicate configure.py Object entries for many source files that were listed once as Object(Matching, ...) and again as Object(NonMatching, ...). The retained entry in the shown hunks is generally the NonMatching one, indicating these files were not to be counted/configured as matching yet.
Affected duplicate removals span multiple configured libraries/systems, including mp, ft, gm, gr, mn, it, if, vi, MSL, dolphin dvd/pad/ar, and sysdolphin baselib.

Examples of duplicate source entries removed include melee/mp/mpcoll.c, melee/ft/ftcoll.c, melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageIce.c, melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c, melee/gr/ground.c, melee/mn/mncharsel.c, melee/it/it_3F14.c, melee/if/ifmagnify.c, melee/vi/vi0402.c, MSL/abort_exit.c, dolphin/dvd/dvd.c, dolphin/pad/pad.c, dolphin/ar/ar.c, and sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c.
Reformatted the import from tools.project into a parenthesized multi-line import list.
Simplified VERSIONS from a multi-line list with a numeric comment to VERSIONS = ["GALE01"].
Reflowed a long Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftMasterHand/ftMh_CaptureDamageMasterHand.c") call across multiple lines.
smart_moves: The fix addressed the build metadata at the central source of truth, configure.py, rather than modifying individual source files or object outputs.

When a file appeared as both Matching and NonMatching, the PR removed the duplicate Matching entry and left the NonMatching entry in the diff excerpt, preventing the same source from being classified in two conflicting states.
The cleanup reduced noise in configured object lists without changing decompiled C source, making future progress calculations and build generation less error-prone.
Formatting cleanups made configure.py easier to scan while touching only one file.
decomp_lessons: In configure.py object lists, a source file should not be present twice with different matching states. Keep exactly one Object(...) entry per source path in a given library configuration.
Progress and build-generation metadata are part of decomp correctness: stale Matching entries can make progress reporting or build composition misleading even if source code is unchanged.
When demoting or correcting a file's status, remove the stale Object(Matching, path) instead of leaving both Matching and NonMatching variants.
Large object-list maintenance PRs should stay mechanical and localized; this one changed only configure.py and did not mix in source-level decomp changes.
naming_conventions: Build configuration uses Object(Matching, "path/to/file.c") and Object(NonMatching, "path/to/file.c") to encode the matching status of each translation unit.
Library grouping helpers visible in the diff include MeleeLib(...), RuntimeLib(...), and DolphinLib(...).
The project version list uses the disc/version identifier string "GALE01" in configure.py.
Existing source path casing is preserved, including fighter common files such as ftCo_DamageIce.c and character-specific files such as ftSk_SpecialHi.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was not an assembly or code-generation matching tactic PR; it was a build-configuration cleanup.
The practical matching tactic is to keep matching-status metadata mutually exclusive: do not list a translation unit as both Matching and NonMatching.
For files that are not yet matching, retain only Object(NonMatching, path) so the build/progress tooling reflects the current state.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that changed configure.py object status from NonMatching to Matching for files cleaned up in PR 1081.
Search for any remaining duplicate Object entries in configure.py after PR 1081.
Review tools.project calculate_progress/generate_build behavior around duplicate source paths to understand why duplicates were harmful.
Check whether future configure.py edits preserve one Object entry per source path per library.
